parsed_json.final_answer: CAL-ACR-022 does not satisfy activation dependency for ACT-ACR-2026-8022. No abstract field is genuinely open; each is either concretely mismatched or only operationally satisfied. The device_group field is concretely matched (DG-41B both sides) but per STD-ACR-022-P this alone is insufficient. The site field is concretely mismatched (Site 18-L vs Site 18-P). The use_class field is concretely mismatched (lab vs production). The release_condition field is only operationally satisfied—RC-7 exists for both records but CAL-ACR-022 covers lab units for Site 18-L, not production units for Site 18-P. The timing field is not genuinely open; calibration must precede activation for the SAME device group at the SAME site. NOTE-ACR-022 warns about the shared prefix to prevent exactly this conflation. The prior artifact correctly identified the binding class and preserved all four critical features. The activation dependency remains open; platform operations may not activate until calibration evidence matching Site 18-P production units is attached.

This warning exists precisely to prevent treating shared prefixes as sufficient evidence. The prior artifact correctly identified this warning must not be used to circumvent the mismatch when calibration evidence is for lab units and activation targets production units. The warning supports the ESCALATE verdict rather than undermining it.
